caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied 3-digit 531 mix is materially contaminated by lessors and property managers, not brokerage offices.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Independent-contractor agent commissions are outside W-2 payroll and not included as automatable labor.
inputs.pi_dist: Commission terms and post-settlement repricing vary by market and brokerage.
inputs.pi_moat: NAR member evidence measures agent use, not ownership of lead flow or retention of an acquired office.
inputs.t50_years: This is an estimate of effective operating adoption, not a source-reported forecast; regular REALTOR use is not identical to NAICS 531210 brokerage payroll automation.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: S1 does not label any frequency tier as effective adoption, and its REALTOR respondent scope is not identical to NAICS 531210 firms.
inputs.historical_analogs: Analogue is directional, not a causal forecast.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: This is not a measured NAICS 531210 owner statistic.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of reopened documentation is not evidence that no succession issue exists.
inputs.active_consolidators: No code-specific, current consolidator census was reopened.
inputs.buy_mult: No reopened transaction-comparable source; broker-owner normalization and revenue basis vary.
inputs.exit_mult: No reopened transaction-comparable source; not a claim of observed market pricing.
inputs.pricing_structure: No single broker P&L or post-settlement commission series is used; gross commission and retained company dollar must not be conflated.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.0396 (margins.json[53]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Real Estate (Operations & Services) EBITDA/Sales 3.96% (agency/brokerage/property-management economics, dominant in firm counts)); revenue/employee $432,174 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 531210); class <5: 140,557 firms, avg revenue/firm $449,562 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $17,803 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 6,638 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,862,814 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $113,367 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 2,095 firms, avg revenue/firm $6,465,620 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $256,039 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 1,204 firms, avg revenue/firm $16,484,213 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $652,775 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 259 firms, avg revenue/firm $48,286,429 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,912,143 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 139 firms, avg revenue/firm $373,541,367 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $14,792,238 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 259

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Task estimates use the supplied 3-digit proxy only as a constrained wage-weighting device; AI evidence supports office and marketing automation, while physical and licensed work remains partly human.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 531210 ($30,836,359,913) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 531210 ($180,013,344,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: The exact attempt-2 packet, finalized run and memo pass schema, identity, routing, frozen-digest, dataset, deterministic-rerender and arithmetic checks. Both registered NAR sources are accessible and support the material reported adoption and consumer-agent-use facts. The 42% adoption input is a valid calculation from mutually exclusive reported frequency tiers, while its interpretation as effective firm adoption is candidly disclosed as a proxy. Several buyability, capture and multiple inputs remain low-confidence estimates rather than source-observed values, but their missing code-specific census, owner, transaction-comparable and pricing-series evidence is expressly caveated, ranges are provided, factor semantics remain correct, capture is separated from terminal demand, and the weak-acquisition conclusion remains useful.

sources.title: REALTORS Embrace AI, Digital Tools to Enhance Client Service, NAR Survey Finds
sources.what_it_supports: 2025 REALTOR AI-use frequency (20% daily, 22% weekly, 27% a few times monthly, 32% never), reported business impact, technology use and brokerage support; member-survey scope.
